key_insight: A hypernetwork-conditioned hybrid physics-data surrogate can learn microstructure-dependent mechanical behavior and make multiscale optimization of graded mycelium composites tractable with far less training data.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical design of functionally graded sustainable materials by linking microscale choices and manufacturing variables directly to macroscale stress performance.
method: Train a hybrid physics-data surrogate for microscale mechanics.
Condition the surrogate on microstructural variables using a hypernetwork.
Extend conditioning to manufacturing variables to infer achievable microstructures and optimize graded structures.
evidence_signal: The approach was validated against full FE^2 simulation and reduced peak stress by 42% in an optimized graded disk compared with a random microstructure baseline.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training set spans the available microstructure and manufacturing variability, especially when extrapolating beyond observed conditions.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
